title: Regime-wise OOF stacking with beta or Venn-Abers calibration before blending
summary: Your stack already relies heavily on rank blending, which suggests score-scale mismatch across experts/regimes.
rationale: Your stack already relies heavily on rank blending, which suggests score-scale mismatch across experts/regimes. Beta calibration handles skewed score distortions better than Platt scaling, while Venn-Abers methods are specifically designed to improve probability calibration from OOF-style predictions.
implementation_hint: Train the base experts as usual, then fit calibrators on OOF predictions separately for a few broad regimes such as wet/dry and early/mid/late race; feed calibrated scores into a tiny meta-learner or regime-gated linear blend instead of raw/ranked scores only.
expected_effect: Can turn small but real expert differences into a more stable ensemble, especially when one model is overconfident in specific regimes.
risk: Calibration will not help if models are already nearly rank-equivalent; too many regime splits can overfit and even hurt AUC.
